Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, I need to understand the key activities and their relationships. The activities are OR, MS, QS, AS, IQC, TST, RT, PK, LB, DP, D. Looking at the examples: In Case 1: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS -> IQC -> AS -> TST -> RT -> TST -> PK -> LB -> DP -> D Case 2 skips LB: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS -> IQC -> AS -> TST -> PK -> DP -> D Case 3 has multiple IQC steps: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS -> IQC -> IQC -> AS -> IQC -> AS -> TST -> RT -> TST -> PK -> LB -> DP -> D Case 4 skips IQC and RT entirely: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS -> TST -> PK -> LB -> DP -> D Case 5 has multiple RT steps: ... -> TST -> RT -> TST -> RT -> TST -> ...

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the main sequence seems to start with OR, then MS, QS, then AS. But after AS, theres a loop with IQC. In some cases, IQC is done once, sometimes multiple times. Each time after IQC, it goes back to AS.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So the loop is * (AS, IQC), meaning you do AS, then either exit the loop (to TST) or do IQC and then AS again.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Similarly, for the testing loop: * (TST, RT). Then, after TST (exiting the loop), you go to PK.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: LB is sometimes skipped. In Case 2, LB is skipped. So after PK, theres a choice between LB and a silent transition (skip). So X (LB, skip). Then, after LB or skip, you go to DP, then D. The main sequence is OR -> MS -> QS -> AS loop -> TST loop -> PK -> LB choice -> DP -> D.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Case 4, after QS, they go AS -> TST. So AS_IQC_loop would have AS done once, then exit the loop to TST_RT_loop.

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Wait, but in Case 3: AS -> IQC -> IQC -> AS -> IQC -> AS -> TST. This suggests that the loop * (AS, IQC) as defined may not capture multiple IQCs in a row. According to * (A, B), after A (AS), you can do B (IQC) then A (AS), but not B then B.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Alternative idea: perhaps the loop is on IQC, but with a silent transition. Wait, maybe the loop is * (AS, * (IQC, skip)).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Define B = * (IQC, skip), which allows one or more IQCs. Then, the outer loop * (AS, B) would mean: do AS, then either exit or do B (one or more IQCs) and then AS again. This would allow sequences like AS -> IQC -> IQC -> AS.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Case 3: AS -> B (IQC, IQC) -> AS -> B (IQC) -> AS -> exit. Yes, this fits. Similarly, in Case 1: AS -> B (IQC) -> AS -> exit. In Case 4: AS -> exit.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main StrictPartialOrder will have nodes: OR, MS, QS, AS_IQC_loop, TST_RT_loop, PK, LB_choice, DP, D With order: OR -> MS -> QS -> AS_IQC_loop -> TST_RT_loop -> PK -> LB_choice -> DP -> D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now, check if this model captures all cases. Case 1: yes. Case 2: yes. Case 3: yes. Case 4: yes. Case 5: yes.
